articles.title: Cloud development platform Vercel was hacked
articles.summary: Vercel, a major web development platform, was hacked by ShinyHunters group who stole and are attempting to sell employee data including names and emails. Vercel confirmed the security incident affected a limited subset of users.

articles.title: The RAM shortage could last years
articles.summary: RAM shortage expected to persist until 2027-2030 as suppliers can only meet 60% of demand by end of 2027. Major chipmakers Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron are ramping production but cannot keep pace with growing demand.

articles.title: OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
articles.summary: Bill Peebles, leader of OpenAI's Sora video generation team, is leaving the company after OpenAI discontinued the project. The departure reflects OpenAI's shift in priorities toward coding and enterprise applications.

articles.title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
articles.summary: The NSA reportedly uses Anthropic's restricted Mythos AI model despite tensions with the Pentagon. This reveals government intelligence agencies are leveraging advanced AI systems for classified operations.

articles.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
articles.summary: Fermi, an AI nuclear power startup co-founded by former Energy Secretary Rick Perry, experienced leadership changes as CEO and CFO departed. The company faced challenges with its Texas AI campus project.

articles.title: OpenAI’s existential questions
articles.summary: OpenAI's recent acquisitions are analyzed to determine if they address two major existential challenges facing the company.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs up to $200/month for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative stunt helped recruit engineers and generated significant investor interest.

articles.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
articles.summary: Grinex, a US-sanctioned cryptocurrency exchange, halted operations after a $15 million heist. The exchange claims state-sponsored hackers from 'unfriendly states' targeted Russian users and drained 70 addresses.

articles.title: Recent advances push Big Tech closer to the Q-Day danger zone
articles.summary: Advanced quantum computing progress threatens current cryptographic security. The 2010 Flame malware attack exploited MD5 vulnerabilities, foreshadowing risks from quantum-enabled decryption capabilities.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have attracted thousands of VMware customers fleeing Broadcom's takeover due to dissatisfaction with its strategy. The rival virtualization platform now has approximately 30,000 customers.

articles.title: The Download: murderous ‘mirror’ bacteria, and Chinese workers fighting AI doubles
articles.summary: Scientists proposed creating mirror bacteria with reversed molecular structures for research benefits, but many now fear potential dangers and have reversed their support for the risky project.

articles.title: Colossal Biosciences said it cloned red wolves. Is it for real?
articles.summary: Colossal Biosciences claims to have cloned red wolves, an endangered species once native to eastern Texas. Scientists are investigating the legitimacy of this cloning breakthrough.

articles.title: Chinese tech workers are starting to train their AI doubles–and pushing back
articles.summary: Chinese tech workers are being pressured by employers to train AI agents to replace them, sparking concerns among early adopters. A viral GitHub project highlighted the trend of companies automating worker tasks using AI tools.

articles.title: Building a Fast Multilingual OCR Model with Synthetic Data
articles.summary: Researchers developed a fast multilingual OCR model using synthetic data for training. The approach reduces computational requirements while maintaining accuracy across multiple languages.

articles.title: Ecom-RLVE: Adaptive Verifiable Environments for E-Commerce Conversational Agents
articles.summary: Ecom-RLVE introduces adaptive verifiable environments for training e-commerce conversational agents using reinforcement learning, enabling better performance in real-world retail scenarios.

articles.title: The PR you would have opened yourself
articles.summary: Article discusses how AI can generate pull requests developers would have created themselves, automating routine code contributions and development tasks.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its implications. The conversation explores upcoming developments and trends in the artificial intelligence field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing campaigns.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available for developers and farmers to leverage advanced farming solutions.

articles.title: 7 ways to travel smarter this summer, with help from Google
articles.summary: Google's latest tools help travelers plan trips, find deals, and explore destinations more efficiently this summer.

articles.title: A new way to explore the web with AI Mode in Chrome
articles.summary: Chrome's AI Mode receives upgrades that transform web interaction through artificial intelligence capabilities, enabling users to explore and engage with online content in new ways.

articles.title: New ways to create personalized images in the Gemini app
articles.summary: Nano Banana 2 enables personalized image creation by leveraging your Google Photos and personal context, generating images that uniquely reflect your individual life and experiences.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Google releases Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, an advanced audio model enabling precise control over AI-generated speech through granular audio tags for expressive audio generation.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics ER 1.6 enhances spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding to improve autonomous robotics performance in real-world tasks through advanced embodied reasoning capabilities.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, their most advanced open-source AI models designed for complex reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved capabilities compared to previous versions.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, while discussions covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals in AI development.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance video game graphics in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate photorealistic details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Nvidia.

articles.title: Bobyard 2.0 offers improved takeoffs and unified AI for estimators
articles.summary: Bobyard 2.0 launches with accelerated takeoff workflows and unified AI workbench for construction estimators. The platform reduces manual measurement errors and speeds up project budgeting through integrated materials and costs.

articles.title: How to prepare for and remediate an AI system incident
articles.summary: Most organizations lack clear procedures to quickly halt AI systems during security incidents, with only 21% able to respond within 30 minutes, creating significant risk of uncontrolled damage.

articles.title: Anthropic walks into the White House and Mythos is the reason Washington let it in
articles.summary: Anthropic CEO met with White House officials despite Trump administration recently labeling the company a supply chain risk. The meeting marks a significant political reversal in AI policy.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing reliability challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture designed to improve autonomous robot navigation in complex indoor environments by addressing localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, creating significant supply chain security risks.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for faster, concise answers but weakens their ability to explore alternative solutions on difficult problems, revealing hidden trade-offs.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems built around them emerge as the true competitive advantage. Anthropic's Claude Code leak demonstrates that the harness matters more than the model itself.

articles.title: OpenAI Scales Trusted Access for Cyber Defense With GPT-5.4-Cyber: a Fine-Tuned Model Built for Verified Security Defenders
articles.summary: OpenAI expands its Trusted Access for Cyber program with GPT-5.4-Cyber, a fine-tuned model for verified security defenders. The initiative uses verified identity and tiered access to balance cybersecurity research with preventing malicious use.

articles.title: Moonshot AI and Tsinghua Researchers Propose PrfaaS: A Cross-Datacenter KVCache Architecture that Rethinks How LLMs are Served at Scale
articles.summary: Moonshot AI and Tsinghua researchers introduce PrfaaS, a cross-datacenter architecture that separates LLM prefill and decode operations, achieving 54% higher throughput than traditional approaches by offloading prefill to specialized clusters.

articles.title: Meet OpenMythos: An Open-Source PyTorch Reconstruction of Claude Mythos Where 770M Parameters Match a 1.3B Transformer
articles.summary: OpenMythos is an open-source PyTorch reconstruction attempting to reverse-engineer Claude Mythos architecture. The 770M parameter model theoretically matches a 1.3B transformer's performance based on peer-reviewed research.

articles.title: Apple's foldable iPhone hinges on one key feature for me - and it's not the hardware
articles.summary: Apple's foldable iPhone success depends on improved multitasking capabilities rather than hardware innovation. The company must enhance software to fully leverage the device's potential.

articles.title: T-Mobile is practically giving away the Apple Watch Series 11 - here's how to get one
articles.summary: T-Mobile offers $300 discount on Apple Watch Series 11 with cellular when adding a new watch line. Deal provides significant savings on the latest smartwatch model.

articles.title: I tested DJI's tiny 4K action camera for weeks - and now I'm ditching my GoPro for it
articles.summary: DJI's Osmo Nano action camera offers compact 4K recording, prompting the reviewer to replace their GoPro. Its powerful performance in a tiny form factor makes it ideal for adventure documentation.

articles.title: Getting Started with Zero-Shot Text Classification
articles.summary: Zero-shot text classification enables labeling text without requiring task-specific training data. This approach allows classifiers to work on new tasks immediately.

articles.title: The Complete Guide to Inference Caching in LLMs
articles.summary: Inference caching optimizes LLM API calls by reducing costs and latency at scale. This guide explores techniques to efficiently cache model outputs.

articles.title: Python Decorators for Production Machine Learning Engineering
articles.summary: Article explores using Python decorators in production machine learning engineering. Decorators help streamline ML workflows and improve code organization.

articles.title: Context Payload Optimization for ICL-Based Tabular Foundation Models
articles.summary: This article explores context payload optimization techniques for in-context learning (ICL) based tabular foundation models, providing conceptual frameworks and practical implementation guidance.

articles.title: The LLM Gamble
articles.summary: The article explores why large language models are psychologically engaging for users and examines the implications this has for the AI industry's future development and adoption.

articles.title: From Risk to Asset: Designing a Practical Data Strategy That Actually Works
articles.summary: Organizations can transform data from a risk into a strategic asset by designing practical strategies that enable faster decision-making and reduce uncertainty while advancing business goals.

articles.title: Autonomous AI at Scale: Adobe Agents Unlock Breakthrough Creative Intelligence With NVIDIA and WPP
articles.summary: Adobe and NVIDIA partner with WPP to deploy autonomous AI agents that transform enterprise creative production and marketing operations, accelerating content creation and decision-making at scale.

articles.title: NVIDIA and Partners Showcase the Future of AI-Driven Manufacturing at Hannover Messe 2026
articles.summary: NVIDIA and partners showcase AI-driven manufacturing solutions at Hannover Messe 2026, demonstrating how accelerated computing, AI physics, agents and robotics transform industrial production.

articles.title: No Need for Space Gear — Capcom’s ‘PRAGMATA’ Joins GeForce NOW on Launch Day
articles.summary: Capcom's sci-fi action game PRAGMATA launches on GeForce NOW the same day as its worldwide release, allowing players to stream the lunar adventure on any device without specialized hardware.

articles.title: Omnichannel ordering with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore and Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore and Nova 2 Sonic enable building voice-enabled omnichannel ordering systems across mobile, web, and voice interfaces with bidirectional audio streaming and context management.

articles.title: Introducing granular cost attribution for Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock now offers granular cost attribution for AI inference, automatically tracking costs by IAM principal for improved chargeback and cost optimization across cloud spending.

articles.title: Optimize video semantic search intent with Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Bedrock optimizes video semantic search by balancing accuracy and latency. The approach uses intelligent intent routing to reduce search time from 2-4 seconds while maintaining strong performance.

articles.title: Full-Stack Optimizations for Agentic Inference with NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo enables full-stack optimizations for agentic inference, with AI agents now generating production code at scale across companies like Stripe, Ramp, and Spotify.

articles.title: Build a More Secure, Always-On Local AI Agent with OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw
articles.summary: OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw enable secure, always-on local AI agents that evolve beyond Q&A into autonomous assistants capable of reading files, calling APIs, and managing multi-step workflows.

articles.title: Accelerate Clean, Modular, Nuclear Reactor Design with AI Physics
articles.summary: AI accelerates development of Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) and Generation IV nuclear designs to meet safety, efficiency, and sustainability requirements while improving project economics through standardized construction.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward social media ads across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2030 and is developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint while sharing these innovations openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background leveraged their unique perspective to develop innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive technological breakthroughs.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Research focuses on understanding LLM behavior through interpretability methods like feature attribution to make AI systems more transparent and trustworthy. The work aims to identify interactions at scale to better comprehend complex machine learning decision-making processes.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems using encoders to map objects to images while accounting for noise corruption. An information estimator quantifies measurement quality using only noisy data and noise models.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD's scalability issues and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
